title: Trace Error Rate by Service and Operation (OpenTelemetry ERROR spans, exception events, HTTP and DB semantic conventions, APM bridges, SLO burn-rate versus 99.9% trace success, deploy spike and noisy-neighbour blast-radius cues)
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.5.1 duration-percentile anomaly detection, UC-13.5.3 style trace completeness and orphan-span analytics, pure real-user monitoring without distributed traces, or security detections for malicious trace poisoning. Does not automatically change sampling policies or roll back deployments.
evidence: Saved search obs_uc_13_5_2_trace_error_rate_by_operation with scheduled execution, dashboard drilldowns tied to Splunk APM or trace explorer views, and weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted observability_evidence index.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab tenant, ingest ERROR spans and matching exception events for checkout payment operations with stable service.name tags, confirm obs_uc_13_5_2_trace_error_rate_by_operation returns critical or high severity within two five-minute buckets with non-null owner_team after joins, and verify slo_burn_rate exceeds burn_warn when error_rate is injected above the allowed budget.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After recovery when ERROR spans return to baseline, metrics and Web lanes quiet down, and span volume is steady, confirm severities return to low or medium for tier-one services and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

dataSources: index=spans with sourcetype otel:trace, otel:span, otel:span_events, or jaeger:span for OTLP-normalized spans and exception events; index=metrics, observability_metrics, or otel_metrics for traces.span.errors style counters; index=apm with sourcetype splunk:apm:trace or splunk:apm:span for Observability Cloud APM bridges; index=web with sourcetype access_combined, apache:access, or nginx:access for HTTP 5xx fast-path correlation; index=catalog sourcetype service_inventory for version and environment backfill; KV lookup service_owner.csv keyed on service_name for owner_team routing
app: Splunk Observability Cloud APM bridge, Splunk Add-on for OpenTelemetry Collector (Splunk_TA_otel), optional Splunk CIM Web and Application_State acceleration
spl:
`comment("UC-13.5.2 trace error rate by service and operation — multisearch lanes: span ERROR, exception events, span error metrics, APM bridge, Web 5xx. Tunables: earliest=-4h@h bucket=5m slo_allowed_error_pct=0.1 burn_warn=2")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=spans (sourcetype=otel:trace OR sourcetype=otel:span OR sourcetype=jaeger:span) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name','resource.attributes.service.name',service_name,""))))
      | eval operation=coalesce(name,span_name,'span_name',operation_name,http_route,"unspecified_operation")
      | eval environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,deployment.environment.name,"")
      | eval service_version=coalesce('service.version',service_version,process.runtime.version,"")
      | eval st_raw=lower(toString(coalesce('status.code',status_code,otel_status_code,"")))
      | eval is_err=if(match(st_raw,"error|status_code_error") OR st_raw="2" OR match(st_raw,"^2$"),1,0)
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms,'duration.nanos'/1000000,duration_ns/1000000,0))
      | eval exc_type=if(is_err==1,coalesce('exception.type',exception.type,exception_type,""),"")
      | eval http_st=tonumber(coalesce('http.status_code',http.status_code,http_response_status_code,0))
      | eval db_err=if(match(lower(toString(coalesce('db.system',db_system,""))),"mysql|postgres|cassandra|mssql|oracle")) AND match(lower(toString(coalesce('span.kind',span_kind,""))),"client") AND is_err==1,1,0)
      | eval msg_lane=if(match(lower(toString(coalesce('messaging.system',messaging_system,""))),"kafka|rabbitmq|sqs|pulsar") AND is_err==1,1,0)
      | eval error_count=is_err
      | eval span_count=1
      | where len(service_name)>0
      | fields _time service_name operation environment service_version span_count error_count duration_ms exc_type http_st ]
    [ search index=spans (sourcetype=otel:span_events OR sourcetype=otel:trace) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval evn=lower(toString(coalesce(event_name,event.type,"")))
      | where match(evn,"exception")
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,""))))
      | eval operation=coalesce(span_name,operation,parent_span_name,"exception_event")
      | eval environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,"")
      | eval service_version=coalesce('service.version',service_version,"")
      | eval exc_type=coalesce('exception.type',exception_type,"exception_event")
      | eval http_st=0
      | eval duration_ms=0
      | eval error_count=1
      | eval span_count=1
      | where len(service_name)>0
      | fields _time service_name operation environment service_version span_count error_count duration_ms exc_type http_st ]
    [ search (index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics OR index=otel_metrics) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
        (metric_name=*span*error* OR metric_name=*traces*span*errors* OR metric_name=span_errors_total)
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,service,"otel_metric_service"))))
      | eval operation=coalesce(operation,span_name,operation_name,"metric_operation")
      | eval environment=coalesce(deployment_environment,environment,"")
      | eval service_version=coalesce(service_version,"")
      | eval mv=tonumber(coalesce(value,metric_value,_value,0))
      | eval error_count=if(mv<0,-1*mv,mv)
      | eval span_count=if(error_count>0,error_count*50,1)
      | eval duration_ms=0
      | eval exc_type=""
      | eval http_st=0
      | fields _time service_name operation environment service_version span_count error_count duration_ms exc_type http_st ]
    [ search index=apm (sourcetype="splunk:apm:trace" OR sourcetype=splunk:apm:span) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,'service.name',apm_service,""))))
      | eval operation=coalesce(operation,span_name,apm_operation,"apm_operation")
      | eval environment=coalesce(environment,deployment_environment,"")
      | eval service_version=coalesce(service_version,release_tag,"")
      | eval st2=lower(toString(coalesce(span_status,trace_status,error_type,"ok")))
      | eval is_err=if(match(st2,"error|fail|exception") OR error_flag==1 OR failed==1,1,0)
      | eval sc=tonumber(coalesce(span_count,1))
      | eval error_count=is_err*sc
      | eval span_count=sc
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms,latency_ms,0))
      | eval exc_type=if(is_err==1,coalesce(top_exception,exception_class,""),"")
      | eval http_st=tonumber(coalesce(http_status,0))
      | where len(service_name)>0
      | fields _time service_name operation environment service_version span_count error_count duration_ms exc_type http_st ]
    [ search index=web (sourcetype=access_combined OR sourcetype=apache:access OR sourcetype=nginx:access) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval status_code=tonumber(coalesce(status,http_status_code,0))
      | where status_code>=500
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,http_host,server_name,"web_tier"))))
      | eval operation=strcat(coalesce(http_method,verb,"HTTP")," ",coalesce(uri_path,url_path,"/"))
      | eval environment=coalesce(environment,env,"")
      | eval service_version=coalesce(app_version,"")
      | eval error_count=1
      | eval span_count=1
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(response_time_ms,0))
      | eval exc_type="http_5xx"
      | eval http_st=status_code
      | fields _time service_name operation environment service_version span_count error_count duration_ms exc_type http_st ]
| eval environment=coalesce(environment,"unknown_environment")
| eval service_version=coalesce(service_version,"unknown_version")
| bin _time span=5m
| stats sum(span_count) AS span_count sum(error_count) AS error_count perc95(duration_ms) AS p95_latency_ms
    values(exc_type) AS exc_mv max(http_st) AS top_http_status BY _time service_name operation environment service_version
| eval top_exception_type=coalesce(mvindex(mvfilter(match(exc_mv,".+")),0),mvindex(exc_mv,0),"none_observed")
| eval span_count=if(isnull(span_count) OR span_count<1,1,span_count)
| eval error_rate=round(100*error_count/span_count,4)
| sort 0 + service_name + operation + environment + _time
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f avg(error_rate) AS roll_error_rate_1h BY service_name operation environment service_version
| eventstats avg(error_rate) AS baseline_error_rate BY service_name
| eval baseline_error_rate=coalesce(baseline_error_rate,roll_error_rate_1h,0.0001)
| eval slo_allowed_error_pct=0.1
| eval slo_burn_rate=round(error_rate/nullif(slo_allowed_error_pct,0),4)
| eval severity=case(
    slo_burn_rate>2 AND error_rate>0.05, "critical",
    error_rate>5 AND error_rate>(3*baseline_error_rate), "critical",
    error_rate>(3*baseline_error_rate) AND error_rate>0.5, "high",
    error_rate>(1.5*baseline_error_rate) AND error_rate>0.2, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval severity=case(
    match(severity,"critical|high") AND span_count<30 AND error_rate<1, "medium",
    true(), severity)
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical", "page_oncall_open_trace_explorer_compare_deploy_and_upstream_callers",
    severity=="high", "triage_dependency_graph_validate_db_and_messaging_semantic_tags",
    severity=="medium", "notify_service_owner_review_tail_sampling_and_exception_cardinality",
    true(), "record_trend_only_review_burn_dashboard")
| join type=left max=0 service_name
    [| inputlookup service_owner.csv
     | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(service_name)))
     | fields service_name owner_team ]
| join type=left max=0 service_name
    [ search index=catalog sourcetype=service_inventory earliest=-24h latest=now
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,""))))
      | dedup service_name
      | eval catalog_env=coalesce(environment,stage,"")
      | eval catalog_ver=coalesce(service_version,build_id,"")
      | fields service_name catalog_env catalog_ver ]
| eval environment=coalesce(environment,catalog_env)
| eval service_version=coalesce(service_version,catalog_ver)
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unassigned_platform")
| table _time service_name operation environment service_version span_count error_count error_rate baseline_error_rate top_exception_type top_http_status p95_latency_ms slo_burn_rate severity owner_team recommended_action
description: Detects rising per-operation trace error rates by fusing ERROR status spans, explicit exception span events, span error metrics, Splunk APM bridge rows, and edge HTTP 5xx logs, broken out by service.name, operation or span_name, deployment.environment, and service.version. The control computes error share, rolling windows, service-level baselines, top exception classes, dominant HTTP status when present, tail latency hints, and SLO-style burn-rate against a 99.9 percent trace-success budget, then enriches with ownership and service catalog joins for deploy-time spike and upstream caller investigations.
value: Product and platform leaders see which operations are burning reliability budget before customer complaints aggregate, because error share by operation isolates the exact microservice step that regressed rather than blending everything into a fleet average. Incident teams gain correlated lanes that separate true application failures from partial instrumentation gaps, while finance and risk stakeholders receive explainable evidence tied to named services, versions, and environments instead of opaque red dashboards.
implementation: Land spans and exception events in index=spans, metrics counters in metrics-class indexes, APM bridges in index=apm, and Web access logs in index=web; maintain service_owner.csv and catalog service_inventory for joins. Save obs_uc_13_5_2_trace_error_rate_by_operation on a five-minute cadence with earliest=-4h@h, page when severity is critical or high after volume guards, and route by owner_team. Tune slo_allowed_error_pct, baseline multipliers, and minimum span_count floors per tier-zero operations.
visualization: Heatmap of error_rate by service_name and operation, single-value tiles for critical severity counts, timeline of slo_burn_rate, paired bar chart of error_count versus span_count, drilldown table keyed on top_exception_type and top_http_status with links to trace explorer and deploy markers.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Application_State.info) AS app_info latest(Application_State.severity) AS app_sev count FROM datamodel=Application_State WHERE nodename=Application_State earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Application_State.dest Application_State.app
| rename Application_State.dest AS service_key Application_State.app AS app_key
| join type=left max=0 service_key [| tstats summariesonly=true avg(Web.status) AS avg_http FROM datamodel=Web WHERE nodename=Web Web.status>=500 earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Web.src | rename Web.src AS service_key]
| join type=left max=0 service_key [| tstats summariesonly=true perc95(Performance.total_processor_time) AS p95_cpu FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.process | rename Performance.process AS service_key]
| table service_key app_key app_info app_sev avg_http p95_cpu
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Application_State summaries when APM health rows map into Application_State for service keys tied to service.name; accelerate Web summaries for edge HTTP status and URL facets used alongside trace HTTP attributes; accelerate Performance summaries when correlating host saturation with client span error storms.

references.title: OpenTelemetry — Semantic conventions for HTTP spans
Splunk Docs — Introduction to Splunk APM
Google SRE — Workbook: Alerting on SLOs
Honeycomb — Error budgets are for problem solving, not blame

knownFalsePositives: Synthetic test traffic that deliberately exercises failure paths can elevate error_rate without customer impact; segregate environments with deployment.environment filters and inventory tiers before paging. Chaos engineering drills and game days inject controlled faults that look like production regressions until you read experiment metadata; tie alerts to change calendars and labeled namespaces. Third-party API maintenance windows that return503 responses can spike HTTP-oriented lanes while your code is healthy; track vendor status pages and map dependency names to suppressible routes. Unsampled high-cardinality endpoints such as ad-hoc debug routes may emit noisy exception types that dominate top_exception_type while volume is tiny; enforce minimum span_count floors and cardinality caps at the collector. Kubernetes NodeNotReady events during pod evictions can interrupt sidecar exports and create bursts of ERROR spans that self-heal when the node drains; correlate with kube events before rollback. Canary deployments that send a sliver of traffic through experimental code paths routinely lift error_share for narrow operations; compare rollouts and feature flags. Client-side retries after benign timeouts can duplicate error spans when idempotency is imperfect; examine messaging and HTTP client policies. Load balancer misconfiguration that returns502 for health checks might flood Web lanes without matching server spans; validate probe paths separately. Partial trace ingestion gaps can make denominators shrink and error_rate appear inflated; cross-check raw event volume and forwarder health. Security scanners hammering edge URLs may generate 5xx rows that should not page application teams; maintain scanner allowlists and edge-only routing.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control together with observability engineers, service owners, and site reliability leads who steward distributed tracing quality across Splunk Observability Cloud application performance views and Splunk Enterprise indexes that receive OpenTelemetry Protocol spans, Jaeger thrift exports, Zipkin JSON, or vendor APM bridges. Before you tune burn-rate thresholds, align on what counts as a failed span: OpenTelemetry marks spans with status code ERROR, records exception events with typed attributes, and maps HTTP semantic conventions so that client and server spans can disagree unless you reconcile them deliberately. Database client spans should carry db.system and db.operation so timeout cascades read as data-plane failures rather than mystery internal errors. Messaging producers and consumers should populate messaging.system and messaging.destination so Kafka rebalance loops do not masquerade as application exceptions. W3C Trace Context explains how trace identifiers propagate; if propagation breaks, error rates on individual services can look healthy while user journeys fail upstream.

This narrative is deliberately error-first and rate-first. UC-13.5.1 remains the authoritative home for percentile dwell on duration tails, slow dependency hotspots, and tail-sampling drift that changes which slow traces survive. UC-13.5.2 instead asks whether operations are failing, how fast failure share is rising versus a calm baseline, and whether you are consuming error budget faster than a 99.9 percent trace-success objective allows. If duration alarms dominate without error movement, start in UC-13.5.1. If errors spike while latency percentiles look flat, stay here and validate semantic conventions before blaming hardware.

Instrument microservices with OpenTelemetry SDKs that set service.name, service.version, and deployment.environment on the resource, and ensure span names follow stable operation identifiers rather than raw URLs with unbounded cardinality. For head sampling, decide early inclusion and accept that rare failures may never reach storage; for tail sampling, keep enough error and high-latency traces by policy so error-rate math remains representative. A practical compromise keeps always-on recording for ERROR status while probabilistically downsampling OK spans. Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector pipelines should run attribute processors that strip personally identifiable information, normalize http.route, and copy exception.stacktrace only when legal review approves retention.

Bridge spans into the Application_State data model when APM summaries land as structured events: map dest or a synthetic key from service.name, set state to error ratios or health scores calculated offline, and keep info fields for top exception classes. Web data model lanes remain relevant for edge 5xx detection that may predate full tracing on a path. Performance summaries help when you correlate host saturation with rising client span errors even though application logs stay quiet.

Service level objectives framed as trace success, for example 99.9 percent of traces without ERROR status on tier-one operations, imply a tiny allowed error share. Translate objectives into burn-rate windows: compare short-window error share against the budget implied by the SLO target and escalate when consumption is multiplicative within an hour. Pair objective math with deploy markers so version skew and canary steps are visible when error rates jump.

Legal and privacy review must cover exception messages and SQL snippets that can appear in span events; hash or truncate at the collector when counsel requires minimization. Role-based access control should restrict high-cardinality trace indexes to engineering identities with job need, segregate production from staging using deployment.environment, and avoid exporting internal tenant identifiers into shared dashboards when you build per-tenant breakdowns in separate workspaces with duplicated saved searches.

Risk briefing for leadership: a rising error rate on one operation can reflect a noisy neighbour blasting shared dependencies, a partial fleet rollout with mixed binaries, or a third-party API throwing429 and429-like payloads that your instrumentation maps into errors. Sampling changes can alter apparent rates; corroborate with raw counts and alternate lanes in this multisearch before declaring customer impact.

Differentiation recap: per-operation error share with semantic enrichment, multi-lane fusion across spans, span events, metrics, APM bridges, and edge HTTP 5xx, rolling windows with streamstats, fleet baselines with eventstats, SLO burn-rate hints, inventory and ownership joins, deploy-correlation guidance, and tenant-aware analytics. It is not UC-13.5.1 duration percentile outlier detection, not orphan-span completeness analytics, and not pure infrastructure CPU charts without trace context.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A lands raw spans in index=spans with sourcetypes such as otel:trace or otel:span after your mapping layer flattens OTLP resource and span fields. Preserve status.code, span name, parent span identifiers, duration fields, http.status_code, db.system, messaging.system, and exception attributes. If you dual-write to Observability Cloud and Splunk Enterprise, keep field names aligned through macros so the same saved search runs in both estates.

Lane B lands explicit span events or span-attached logs that record exception events with event_name or semantic exception.type. Ensure timestamps align to span boundaries so five-minute rollups do not double-count the same failure across lanes; when double counting is possible, lower severity weights for the exception lane in governance review rather than omitting it.

Lane C pulls metricized counters such as traces.span.errors or exporter equivalents from index=metrics, observability_metrics, or otel_metrics. Normalize service and operation labels to the same lowercase keys used in Lane A. When only error counters exist without denominators, pair them with span volume counters from the same scrape or derive conservative denominators until a better signal arrives.

Lane D ingests Splunk APM bridge rows with sourcetype splunk:apm:trace or splunk:apm:span when Observability Cloud or on-prem APM exports structured traces. Align error_flag or span_status fields to the same is_err logic used for OpenTelemetry.

Lane E provides an HTTP fast path using Web access logs in index=web with sourcetype access_combined, apache:access, or nginx:access, filtering status codes at or above 500. This lane catches edge failures that might not yet emit server spans if instrumentation is partial, and it complements trace-derived HTTP attributes when load balancers terminate TLS.

Configure HTTP Event Collector tokens or OTLP receivers with rate awareness; noisy clients that retry on 500 can inflate both span errors and access log rows, which this control treats as correlated evidence rather than independent proof. Kubernetes annotations should record build identifiers that map to service.version for mixed-version detection during rolling updates.

Example collector checks: verify tail-sampling policies retain ERROR spans, verify batch processors do not drop spans silently on backpressure, and verify attribute limits do not strip status.code while preserving http.status_code. Document macros for index and sourcetype overrides per tenant.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as obs_uc_13_5_2_trace_error_rate_by_operation with a five-minute or fifteen-minute schedule depending on paging culture, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after volume guards. Throttle duplicate service_name and operation pairs for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action and owner_team in pager text so responders open trace explorer, dependency maps, and deploy dashboards without ad hoc guesswork.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans five arms for ERROR spans, structured exception events, span error metrics, APM bridge rows, and Web 5xx access logs. coalesce ladders unify environment and version dimensions when resource attributes are missing. bin enforces five-minute buckets. stats aggregates span_count, error_count, exception cardinality hints, and p95 latency per tuple. streamstats builds a rolling one-hour style average of error_rate per tuple. eventstats estimates baseline_error_rate per service for the search window as a calm reference. case encodes severity with explicit multipliers against baseline and burn-rate against a 99.9 percent style allowance. inputlookup service_owner.csv joins owner_team on normalized service_name. A second join against catalog service_inventory adds catalog metadata for environments or versions that only appear in configuration items.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.5.2 trace error rate by service and operation — multisearch lanes: span ERROR, exception events, span error metrics, APM bridge, Web 5xx. Tunables: earliest=-4h@h bucket=5m slo_allowed_error_pct=0.1 burn_warn=2")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=spans (sourcetype=otel:trace OR sourcetype=otel:span OR sourcetype=jaeger:span) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name','resource.attributes.service.name',service_name,""))))
      | eval operation=coalesce(name,span_name,'span_name',operation_name,http_route,"unspecified_operation")
      | eval environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,deployment.environment.name,"")
      | eval service_version=coalesce('service.version',service_version,process.runtime.version,"")
      | eval st_raw=lower(toString(coalesce('status.code',status_code,otel_status_code,"")))
      | eval is_err=if(match(st_raw,"error|status_code_error") OR st_raw="2" OR match(st_raw,"^2$"),1,0)
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms,'duration.nanos'/1000000,duration_ns/1000000,0))
      | eval exc_type=if(is_err==1,coalesce('exception.type',exception.type,exception_type,""),"")
      | eval http_st=tonumber(coalesce('http.status_code',http.status_code,http_response_status_code,0))
      | eval db_err=if(match(lower(toString(coalesce('db.system',db_system,""))),"mysql|postgres|cassandra|mssql|oracle")) AND match(lower(toString(coalesce('span.kind',span_kind,""))),"client") AND is_err==1,1,0)
      | eval msg_lane=if(match(lower(toString(coalesce('messaging.system',messaging_system,""))),"kafka|rabbitmq|sqs|pulsar") AND is_err==1,1,0)
      | eval error_count=is_err
      | eval span_count=1
      | where len(service_name)>0
      | fields _time service_name operation environment service_version span_count error_count duration_ms exc_type http_st ]
    [ search index=spans (sourcetype=otel:span_events OR sourcetype=otel:trace) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval evn=lower(toString(coalesce(event_name,event.type,"")))
      | where match(evn,"exception")
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,""))))
      | eval operation=coalesce(span_name,operation,parent_span_name,"exception_event")
      | eval environment=coalesce('deployment.environment',deployment_environment,"")
      | eval service_version=coalesce('service.version',service_version,"")
      | eval exc_type=coalesce('exception.type',exception_type,"exception_event")
      | eval http_st=0
      | eval duration_ms=0
      | eval error_count=1
      | eval span_count=1
      | where len(service_name)>0
      | fields _time service_name operation environment service_version span_count error_count duration_ms exc_type http_st ]
    [ search (index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics OR index=otel_metrics) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
        (metric_name=*span*error* OR metric_name=*traces*span*errors* OR metric_name=span_errors_total)
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,service,"otel_metric_service"))))
      | eval operation=coalesce(operation,span_name,operation_name,"metric_operation")
      | eval environment=coalesce(deployment_environment,environment,"")
      | eval service_version=coalesce(service_version,"")
      | eval mv=tonumber(coalesce(value,metric_value,_value,0))
      | eval error_count=if(mv<0,-1*mv,mv)
      | eval span_count=if(error_count>0,error_count*50,1)
      | eval duration_ms=0
      | eval exc_type=""
      | eval http_st=0
      | fields _time service_name operation environment service_version span_count error_count duration_ms exc_type http_st ]
    [ search index=apm (sourcetype="splunk:apm:trace" OR sourcetype=splunk:apm:span) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,'service.name',apm_service,""))))
      | eval operation=coalesce(operation,span_name,apm_operation,"apm_operation")
      | eval environment=coalesce(environment,deployment_environment,"")
      | eval service_version=coalesce(service_version,release_tag,"")
      | eval st2=lower(toString(coalesce(span_status,trace_status,error_type,"ok")))
      | eval is_err=if(match(st2,"error|fail|exception") OR error_flag==1 OR failed==1,1,0)
      | eval sc=tonumber(coalesce(span_count,1))
      | eval error_count=is_err*sc
      | eval span_count=sc
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms,latency_ms,0))
      | eval exc_type=if(is_err==1,coalesce(top_exception,exception_class,""),"")
      | eval http_st=tonumber(coalesce(http_status,0))
      | where len(service_name)>0
      | fields _time service_name operation environment service_version span_count error_count duration_ms exc_type http_st ]
    [ search index=web (sourcetype=access_combined OR sourcetype=apache:access OR sourcetype=nginx:access) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval status_code=tonumber(coalesce(status,http_status_code,0))
      | where status_code>=500
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,http_host,server_name,"web_tier"))))
      | eval operation=strcat(coalesce(http_method,verb,"HTTP")," ",coalesce(uri_path,url_path,"/"))
      | eval environment=coalesce(environment,env,"")
      | eval service_version=coalesce(app_version,"")
      | eval error_count=1
      | eval span_count=1
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(response_time_ms,0))
      | eval exc_type="http_5xx"
      | eval http_st=status_code
      | fields _time service_name operation environment service_version span_count error_count duration_ms exc_type http_st ]
| eval environment=coalesce(environment,"unknown_environment")
| eval service_version=coalesce(service_version,"unknown_version")
| bin _time span=5m
| stats sum(span_count) AS span_count sum(error_count) AS error_count perc95(duration_ms) AS p95_latency_ms
    values(exc_type) AS exc_mv max(http_st) AS top_http_status BY _time service_name operation environment service_version
| eval top_exception_type=coalesce(mvindex(mvfilter(match(exc_mv,".+")),0),mvindex(exc_mv,0),"none_observed")
| eval span_count=if(isnull(span_count) OR span_count<1,1,span_count)
| eval error_rate=round(100*error_count/span_count,4)
| sort 0 + service_name + operation + environment + _time
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f avg(error_rate) AS roll_error_rate_1h BY service_name operation environment service_version
| eventstats avg(error_rate) AS baseline_error_rate BY service_name
| eval baseline_error_rate=coalesce(baseline_error_rate,roll_error_rate_1h,0.0001)
| eval slo_allowed_error_pct=0.1
| eval slo_burn_rate=round(error_rate/nullif(slo_allowed_error_pct,0),4)
| eval severity=case(
    slo_burn_rate>2 AND error_rate>0.05, "critical",
    error_rate>5 AND error_rate>(3*baseline_error_rate), "critical",
    error_rate>(3*baseline_error_rate) AND error_rate>0.5, "high",
    error_rate>(1.5*baseline_error_rate) AND error_rate>0.2, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval severity=case(
    match(severity,"critical|high") AND span_count<30 AND error_rate<1, "medium",
    true(), severity)
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical", "page_oncall_open_trace_explorer_compare_deploy_and_upstream_callers",
    severity=="high", "triage_dependency_graph_validate_db_and_messaging_semantic_tags",
    severity=="medium", "notify_service_owner_review_tail_sampling_and_exception_cardinality",
    true(), "record_trend_only_review_burn_dashboard")
| join type=left max=0 service_name
    [| inputlookup service_owner.csv
     | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(service_name)))
     | fields service_name owner_team ]
| join type=left max=0 service_name
    [ search index=catalog sourcetype=service_inventory earliest=-24h latest=now
      | eval service_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,""))))
      | dedup service_name
      | eval catalog_env=coalesce(environment,stage,"")
      | eval catalog_ver=coalesce(service_version,build_id,"")
      | fields service_name catalog_env catalog_ver ]
| eval environment=coalesce(environment,catalog_env)
| eval service_version=coalesce(service_version,catalog_ver)
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unassigned_platform")
| table _time service_name operation environment service_version span_count error_count error_rate baseline_error_rate top_exception_type top_http_status p95_latency_ms slo_burn_rate severity owner_team recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch for packaging teams:

```ini
[obs_uc_13_5_2_trace_error_rate_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch obs_uc_13_5_2_trace_error_rate_by_operation | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance guidance: when Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time climbing, summarize each lane into five-minute rollup summaries keyed on service_name and operation, widen alerts to read summaries with earliest=-2h@h, and retain this full search for investigations. For very large fleets, split alerts by environment using where clauses driven by lookup tier columns.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in your trace explorer: pick a service with known failures, compare visual error share for a single operation to the Splunk row in the same bucket, and reconcile field normalization if counts diverge. Validate exception event lanes against span status to ensure you are not triple-counting the same failure; adjust span_count and error_count weighting in workshops if needed.

Validate metrics arms by comparing traces.span.errors style counters to manual sums of ERROR spans for a narrow window in a lab tenant. If metrics exceed span counts, check for duplicated scrapes or different label cardinalities.

Validate Web lane alignment by correlating 5xx access log spikes with server span errors; mismatches often reveal incomplete instrumentation on edge paths or mis-tuned load balancer logging.

Validate joins by running inputlookup service_owner.csv alone and confirming keys cover tier-zero services. Validate catalog inventory by ensuring deduped service_name rows exist for newly onboarded workloads.

Synthetic drill: inject controlled ERROR spans in non-production, confirm severity escalates within two buckets, then restore and confirm return toward low severity with stable baselines.

Negative test: on a quiet window without deploys, rows should remain low severity for high-volume services unless baselines are miscalibrated; if noise persists, raise span_count floors in case logic.

Runbook linkage: tie results to change calendars, feature-flag consoles, database incident playbooks, and messaging cluster health dashboards.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Cold-deploy spike: error_rate jumps immediately after a rollout while duration stays flat; compare mixed service.version values, validate readiness probes, and roll forward or back based on canary health.

Case 2 — Sidecar OOM: collector or proxy sidecars restart, producing bursts of export failures and apparent span errors; inspect Kubernetes memory limits and OTel pipeline queues before blaming application code.

Case 3 — Service mesh mTLS rotation breakage: ERROR spans appear on outbound clients with handshake failures; align trust bundles, rotation windows, and destination rule subsets.

Case 4 — Downstream DB timeout cascade: db client spans spike errors while pool metrics show saturation; throttle aggressive retries and coordinate with data store owners on capacity.

Case 5 — Malformed schema rollout: a new protobuf or JSON field makes parsers throw, elevating exception events; pin consumers, add compatibility shims, and gate deploys with contract tests.

Case 6 — Kafka rebalance loop: messaging spans show repeated consume errors; inspect broker health, consumer group stability, and partition counts before scaling services blindly.

Case 7 — Retry storms: client spans multiply errors after upstream flicker; tune retry budgets, jitter, and idempotency keys while watching burn-rate decay.

Case 8 — Feature-flag toggle bug: a flag flips exception paths for a slice of traffic; correlate flag audit logs with operation-level error_rate lifts.

Case 9 — gRPC health-check false errors: health RPCs marked ERROR despite ready workloads; separate health endpoints from business RPC instrumentation or filter health paths in alerting.

Case 10 — Partial deploy with mixed versions: streamstats shows divergent error_rate by service_version; accelerate rollout completion or pin traffic away from bad binaries.

Case 11 — Library upgrade introducing exception type: top_exception_type shifts suddenly without traffic change; review release notes, bytecode agents, and dynamic instrumentation.

Case 12 — Third-party API rate limit: HTTP client spans show 429-to-error mappings; negotiate quotas, add backoff, and document vendor maintenance that mirrors customer pain.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, and inputlookup appear; final table lists required operational columns including owner_team and recommended_action.
grandmaExplanation: We treat each online request like a package crossing many conveyor belts. When too many boxes get stamped rejected at one station, we spot which belt and which worker step caused the pile-up, compare it with the usual rejection rate, and raise the alarm before shoppers wonder why deliveries slowed.
hardware: Kubernetes-hosted microservices, Linux service instances, OpenTelemetry Collector gateways or agents, optional service mesh proxies, Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search tier at 9.2 or newer indexing trace and metric telemetry.
